title: cochlidiosperma cymbalaria
intro: Cochlidiosperma cymbalaria, also known as "Spanish eyes" or "toad's eye," is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Herbal preparations of Cochlidiosperma cymbalaria include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
